title: [InstCombine] Opt phi(freeze(undef), C) -> phi(C, C)
summary: The generalization strategy identifies a core pattern—a PHI node with a freeze(undef) operand and constant incoming values—and extracts it into a reusable helper function. The original test case handled only a specific scenario where all non-freeze operands were identical constants (0 or 1). The patch generalizes this to detect any common constant across all non-freeze PHI operands, ensuring they are guaranteed not to be undef or poison. This enables the optimization to apply to mixed constants (if they match), vectors, and cases with multiple freeze operands, while correctly rejecting PHIs with non-constant values. The approach modularizes the logic into `pickCommonConstantFromPHI`, making it easy to extend and test independently. Developers should isolate such patterns into focused helpers that validate preconditions precisely, enabling robust, broader transformations.
